message.content:
You are executing a bridled pace for Job Jockey.

## Pace: trophy-alpha-jjsa-commit-types (₢AFAA0)

## Spec

Update JJSA spec with new commit types and parameter for trophy data collection.

### Additions to JJSA

#### Commit Types Section

Add to "Commit Message Patterns" section:

**B (Bridle)** — Records bridling decision before autonomous execution
- Created when pace transitions to bridled state
- Subject: `jjb:HALLMARK:₢XXXXX:B: {agent} | {file_count} files | {silks}`
- Body contains full direction text

**L (Landing)** — Records agent return after autonomous execution
- Created when spawned agent completes (success or failure)
- Subject: `jjb:HALLMARK:₢XXXXX:L: {agent} {landed|crashed} | {steps_completed}/{steps_total} | verify: {pass|fail}`
- Body contains files touched, duration, error if crashed

#### Arguments Section

Add `--intent` argument for jjx_notch:
- Optional string parameter
- If provided, used as commit subject (opus-supplied intent)
- If omitted, falls back to haiku diff description

## File

- Tools/jjk/vov_veiled/JJSA-GallopsData.adoc

## Direction

1. Read existing Commit Message Patterns section
2. Add B Bridle marker definition after existing markers
3. Add L Landing marker definition
4. Add --intent argument to jjx_notch Arguments section

## Wrap Discipline

DO NOT run /jjc-pace-wrap or mark the pace complete. When work is done:
1. Verify: visual review of adoc formatting
2. Report completion status
3. STOP — the calling agent will confirm wrap with the user

   150→
   151→== Overview
   152→
   153→The
   154→{jjdgr_gallops}
   155→is the persistent data store for Job Jockey.
   156→It tracks
   157→{jjdhr_heat_s}
   158→(bounded initiatives) and their constituent
   159→{jjdpr_pace_s}
   160→(discrete actions).
   161→Each
   162→{jjdpr_pace}
   163→maintains a history of
   164→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   165→(plan refinements).
   166→
   167→== Design Principles
   168→
   169→=== Exclusive JSON Ownership
   170→
   171→All access to the
   172→{jjdgr_gallops}
   173→goes through {jjdx_cli}, the Job Jockey subcommand of the Voce Viva executor.
   174→No jq or direct JSON manipulation ever touches this data.
   175→This is an absolute architectural constraint, not a preference.
   176→
   177→The rationale: writing reliable bash with jq pipelines requires fighting LLM training data.
   178→The internet is full of bad bash.
   179→Rust replaces fragile jq invocations with compiler-enforced correctness.
   180→The compiler is tireless; humans are not.
   181→
   182→=== Git as Journal
   183→
   184→The
   185→{jjdgr_gallops}
   186→remains JSON specifically so git can serve as the journalling layer.
   187→Every mutation creates a diffable commit.
   188→Git owns the complete history; we need not implement our own audit trail.
   189→
   190→The
   191→{jjdkr_tack}
   192→history within each
   193→{jjdpr_pace}
   194→exists for convenient postmortem analysis.
   195→Git has the full history, but
   196→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   197→let us see plan evolution without spelunking commits.
   198→
   199→=== Diff-Friendly Mutations
   200→
   201→New
   202→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   203→prepend to position 0 rather than append.
   204→This produces cleaner git diffs: prepending adds a line with trailing comma, while appending would modify the previous last line to add a comma.
   205→Small discipline, large cumulative benefit.
   206→
   207→The
   208→{jjdhm_order}
   209→array contains full
   210→{jjdt_coronet_s}.
   211→Each
   212→{jjdt_coronet}
   213→embeds its parent
   214→{jjdt_firemark}
   215→(redundant within a single
   216→{jjdhr_heat}).
   217→This redundancy is cheap and enables grep, copy-paste, and self-documenting JSON.
   218→
   219→=== Crash-Safe Architecture
   220→
   221→Bash handles locking via `git update-ref`.
   222→{jjdx_cli} owns all
   223→{jjdgr_gallops}
   224→file access - reading, transforming, and writing.
   225→{jjdx_cli} implements atomic writes internally (write to temp file, then rename).
   226→If {jjdx_cli} crashes mid-transform, no partial writes occur.
   227→
   228→The pattern:
   229→[source,bash]
   230→----
   231→lock_acquire
   232→vvx jjx gallops nominate --file gallops.json "my-heat" "260111" || { lock_release; die; }
   233→lock_release
   234→----
   235→
   236→This keeps bash simple (just locking and invocation) while {jjdx_cli} owns all JSON file operations.
   237→
   238→=== Deterministic Serialization
   239→
   240→Implementations MUST serialize JSON object keys in deterministic order to minimize diff churn.
   241→For `heats` and `paces` objects, use sorted key order (e.g., Rust `BTreeMap` instead of `HashMap`).
   242→This ensures that reordering paces via
   243→{jjdo_rail}
   244→produces minimal diffs — only the `order` array changes, not the entire `paces` object.
   245→
   246→=== Identity Encoding
   247→
   248→{jjdt_firemark}
   249→(`₣` prefix) and
   250→{jjdt_coronet}
   251→(`₢` prefix) are the canonical identities used in JSON keys, CLI arguments, and display.
   252→The base64 encoding provides compact, URL-safe, shell-safe strings.
   253→Internally, Rust may decode to integers for arithmetic, but the string form is authoritative.
   254→
   255→=== Horse Racing Vocabulary
   256→
   257→Job Jockey uses horse racing metaphors throughout.
   258→This is intentional: vivid domain vocabulary helps humans hold complex systems in working memory.
   259→
   260→* {jjdhr_heat} - a bounded initiative (like a race)
   261→* {jjdpr_pace} - a discrete action (the rhythm of the race)
   262→* {jjdkr_tack} - plan refinement (equipment; also "tacking" = adjusting course)
   263→* {jjdt_firemark} - heat identity, `₣`-prefixed (hot-iron brand marking ownership)
   264→* {jjdt_coronet} - pace identity, `₢`-prefixed (marking at the hoof line)
   265→* {jjdt_silks} - display name (jockey's colors)
   266→* {jjdgr_gallops} - the training grounds (where real work happens before race day)
   267→
   268→== Types
   269→
   270→[[jjdt_firemark]]
   271→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   272→{jjdt_firemark}::
   273→The identity of a
   274→{jjdhr_heat}.
   275→Format: `₣` + 2 base64 characters (e.g., `₣AB`).
   276→The 2 base64 characters encode an integer 0-4095 (2^12).
   277→Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
   278→Input may omit the `₣` prefix; output always includes it.
   279→
   280→[[jjdt_coronet]]
   281→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   282→{jjdt_coronet}::
   283→The identity of a
   284→{jjdpr_pace}.
   285→Format: `₢` + 5 base64 characters (e.g., `₢ABCDE`).
   286→Globally unique: the first 2 base64 characters are the parent
   287→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   288→encoded identity; the remaining 3 encode the pace index (0-262143, 2^18).
   289→Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
   290→Input may omit the `₢` prefix; output always includes it.
   291→+
   292→*Reassignment:* When a
   293→{jjdpr_pace}
   294→is moved between
   295→{jjdhr_heat_s}
   296→via
   297→{jjdo_draft},
   298→the old
   299→{jjdt_coronet}
   300→becomes invalid and a new one is assigned using the destination
   301→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   302→{jjdhm_seed}.
   303→The new
   304→{jjdt_coronet}
   305→embeds the destination
   306→{jjdt_firemark}.
   307→{jjdkr_tack}
   308→history is preserved, with a note recording the original
   309→{jjdt_coronet}.
   310→
   311→[[jjdt_silks]]
   312→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   313→{jjdt_silks}::
   314→A kebab-case display name.
   315→Used for human-readable identification of
   316→{jjdhr_heat_s}
   317→and
   318→{jjdpr_pace_s}.
   319→Pattern: `[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*`
   320→
   321→== Records
   322→
   323→=== {jjdgr_gallops}
   324→
   325→[[jjdgr_gallops]]
   326→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   327→{jjdgr_gallops}::
   328→The root JSON structure containing all Job Jockey data.
   329→Singleton record persisted at `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.
   330→
   331→==== Members
   332→
   333→* [[jjdgm_seed]]
   334→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   335→{jjdgm_seed}
   336→is the next
   337→{jjdt_firemark}
   338→to allocate, stored as 2 base64 characters.
   339→Incremented by
   340→{jjdo_nominate}.
   341→
   342→=== {jjdhr_heat}
   343→
   344→[[jjdhr_heat]]
   345→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   346→{jjdhr_heat}::
   347→A bounded initiative with coherent goals.
   348→Contains zero or more
   349→{jjdpr_pace_s}
   350→representing discrete actions.
   351→Keyed in JSON by
   352→{jjdt_firemark}.
   353→
   354→==== Members
   355→
   356→* [[jjdhm_firemark]]
   357→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   358→{jjdhm_firemark}
   359→is the
   360→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   361→primary key.
   362→Type:
   363→{jjdt_firemark}.
   364→Implicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.
   365→
   366→* [[jjdhm_silks]]
   367→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   368→{jjdhm_silks}
   369→is the
   370→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   371→display name.
   372→Type:
   373→{jjdt_silks}.
   374→
   375→* [[jjdhm_created]]
   376→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   377→{jjdhm_created}
   378→records when the
   379→{jjdhr_heat}
   380→was nominated.
   381→Format: YYMMDD.
   382→
   383→* [[jjdhm_status]]
   384→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   385→{jjdhm_status}
   386→indicates the
   387→{jjdhr_heat_p}
   388→lifecycle state.
   389→Values:
   390→{jjdhe_racing},
   391→{jjdhe_stabled},
   392→or
   393→{jjdhe_retired}.
   394→
   395→* [[jjdhm_order]]
   396→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_repeated⟧
   397→{jjdhm_order}
   398→lists
   399→{jjdt_coronet_s}
   400→in execution sequence.
   401→May be reordered via
   402→{jjdo_rail}.
   403→
   404→* [[jjdhm_seed]]
   405→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   406→{jjdhm_seed}
   407→is the next
   408→{jjdt_coronet}
   409→index to allocate, stored as 3 base64 characters.
   410→Incremented by
   411→{jjdo_slate}.
   412→
   413→* [[jjdhm_paddock]]
   414→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   415→{jjdhm_paddock}
   416→is the path to the paddock file containing
   417→{jjdhr_heat}
   418→context and background.
   419→Format: `.claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md` where XX is the base64-encoded
   420→{jjdt_firemark}
   421→(without `₣` prefix).
   422→Created by
   423→{jjdo_nominate}.
   424→
   425→==== Status Values
   426→
   427→[[jjdhe_racing]]
   428→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   429→{jjdhe_racing}::
   430→The
   431→{jjdhr_heat}
   432→is actively being worked.
   433→
   434→[[jjdhe_stabled]]
   435→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   436→{jjdhe_stabled}::
   437→The
   438→{jjdhr_heat}
   439→is paused, not actively worked.
   440→
   441→[[jjdhe_retired]]
   442→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧
   443→{jjdhe_retired}::
   444→The
   445→{jjdhr_heat}
   446→is complete and archived.
   447→
   448→=== {jjdpr_pace}
   449→
   450→[[jjdpr_pace]]
   451→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   452→{jjdpr_pace}::
   453→A discrete action within a
   454→{jjdhr_heat}.
   455→Contains one or more
   456→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   457→representing state snapshots and plan refinements.
   458→Current state is derived from `tacks[0].state`.
   459→Current silks is derived from `tacks[0].silks`.
   460→Keyed in JSON by
   461→{jjdt_coronet}.
   462→
   463→==== Members
   464→
   465→* [[jjdpm_coronet]]
   466→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   467→{jjdpm_coronet}
   468→is the
   469→{jjdpr_pace_p}
   470→globally unique identity.
   471→Type:
   472→{jjdt_coronet}.
   473→Implicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.
   474→
   475→=== {jjdkr_tack}
   476→
   477→[[jjdkr_tack]]
   478→// ⟦axl_voices axr_record_json⟧
   479→{jjdkr_tack}::
   480→A snapshot of
   481→{jjdpr_pace}
   482→state and plan at a point in time.
   483→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   484→form an append-only history capturing both plan refinements and state transitions.
   485→Position 0 is the most recent; older
   486→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   487→follow.
   488→New
   489→{jjdkr_tack_s}
   490→are prepended for cleaner git diffs.
   491→The current
   492→{jjdpr_pace}
   493→state is always
   494→`tacks[0].state`.
   495→
   496→==== Members
   497→
   498→* [[jjdkm_refined]]
   499→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   500→{jjdkm_refined}
   501→records when this
   502→{jjdkr_tack}
   503→was added.
   504→Format: YYMMDD-HHMM.
   505→
   506→* [[jjdkm_state]]
   507→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   508→{jjdkm_state}
   509→indicates the
   510→{jjdpr_pace_p}
   511→progress at this snapshot.
   512→Values:
   513→{jjdpe_rough},
   514→{jjdpe_bridled},
   515→{jjdpe_complete},
   516→or
   517→{jjdpe_abandoned}.
   518→
   519→* [[jjdkm_text]]
   520→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   521→{jjdkm_text}
   522→contains the plan specification.
   523→Non-empty string describing what the
   524→{jjdpr_pace}
   525→should accomplish.
   526→
   527→* [[jjdkm_silks]]
   528→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   529→{jjdkm_silks}
   530→is the
   531→{jjdpr_pace_p}
   532→display name at this point in time.
   533→Type:
   534→{jjdt_silks}.
   535→Enables rename history; current pace silks is derived from `tacks[0].silks`.
   536→
   537→* [[jjdkm_commit]]
   538→// ⟦axl_voices axr_member axd_required⟧
   539→{jjdkm_commit}
   540→is the commit SHA at
   541→{jjdkr_tack}
   542→creation.
   543→Format: 7-character hex string.
   544→Captures repo state when plan was written.
   545→Value `0000000` indicates commit was unavailable at creation time.

   547→* [[jjdkm_direction]]

   550→contains execution guidance for autonomous operation.
   551→Required when
   552→{jjdkm_state}
   553→is
   554→{jjdpe_bridled};
   555→forbidden otherwise.
   556→Describes which agent to dispatch, parallelism strategy, or other execution parameters.

   570→[[jjdpe_bridled]]
   571→// ⟦axl_voices axt_enum_value⟧

   597→==== State Classification Predicates

   658→== Serialization
   659→
   660→[[jjdz_charset]]
   661→{jjdz_charset}::
   662→The 64-character alphabet for identity encoding.
   663→Uses URL-safe base64 (RFC 4648 §5):
   664→`ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789-_`
   665→Position in charset determines numeric value (A=0, B=1, ..., `-`=62, `_`=63).
   666→This alphabet is safe for JSON keys, URLs, shell, git refs, and filenames.
   667→Matches Rust `base64::engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD`.
   668→
   669→[[jjdz_encoding]]
   670→{jjdz_encoding}::
   671→The algorithm for encoding
   672→{jjdt_firemark}
   673→and
   674→{jjdt_coronet}
   675→values.
   676→+
   677→*{jjdt_firemark} (Heat identity):*
   678→+
   679→. Format: `₣` + 2 base64 characters
   680→. Given integer H (0-4095): encode as `₣` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]`
   681→. To decode `₣XY`: H = pos(X) * 64 + pos(Y)
   682→+
   683→*{jjdt_coronet} (Pace identity):*
   684→+
   685→. Format: `₢` + 5 base64 characters
   686→. First 2 characters encode the parent Heat (same as {jjdt_firemark} without prefix)
   687→. Last 3 characters encode the pace index P (0-262143)
   688→. Given Heat H and pace index P: encode as `₢` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]` + `charset[P/4096]` + `charset[(P/64)%64]` + `charset[P%64]`
   689→. To decode `₢XYZAB`: H from XY, P = pos(Z) * 4096 + pos(A) * 64 + pos(B)
   690→+
   691→*Input flexibility:*
   692→+
   693→CLI commands accept identities with or without prefix.
   694→Length determines type: 2 base64 chars = {jjdt_firemark}, 5 base64 chars = {jjdt_coronet}.
   695→Output always includes the prefix (`₣` or `₢`).

  1049→=== Steeplechase Operations
  1050→
  1051→The steeplechase is Job Jockey's session history, stored in git commits rather than the
  1052→{jjdgr_gallops}
  1053→JSON.
  1054→Commit messages follow patterns that
  1055→{jjdo_rein}
  1056→can parse to reconstruct session history.
  1057→
  1058→==== Commit Message Patterns
  1059→
  1060→All JJ commits use the format: `jjb:HALLMARK:IDENTITY:ACTION: message`
  1061→
  1062→* `jjb` — JJ brand prefix (literal)
  1063→* `HALLMARK` — Version identifier. Format: NNNN (installed) or NNNN-xxxxxxx (dev). Source: `.vvk/vvbf_brand.json` (installed) or max hallmark from `Tools/vok/vov_veiled/vovr_registry.json` + git HEAD (Kit Forge)
  1064→* `IDENTITY` — ₢CORONET for pace-level, ₣FIREMARK for heat-level
  1065→* `ACTION` — single-letter code (required for all commits)
  1066→
  1067→*Standard notch* (created by {jjdo_notch}):
  1068→----
  1069→jjb:HALLMARK:₢XXXXX:n: commit message
  1070→
  1071→Co-Authored-By: ...
  1072→----
  1073→
  1074→Standard notch commits use `n` to denote routine progress tracking.
  1075→
  1076→*Pace-level chalk* (created by {jjdo_chalk}):
  1077→----
  1078→jjb:HALLMARK:₢XXXXX:A: description
  1079→
  1080→Co-Authored-By: ...
  1081→----
  1082→
  1083→Pace-level action codes:
  1084→
  1085→* `n` — Notch: standard commit
  1086→* `A` — APPROACH: proposed approach before work begins
  1087→* `W` — WRAP: pace completion summary
  1088→* `F` — FLY: autonomous execution began (bridled pace)
  1089→* `d` — discussion: significant decision (lowercase, can also be heat-level)
  1090→
  1091→*Heat-level operations*:
  1092→----
  1093→jjb:HALLMARK:₣XX:N: heat-silks
  1094→jjb:HALLMARK:₣XX:S: pace-silks
  1095→jjb:HALLMARK:₣XX:r: reordered
  1096→jjb:HALLMARK:₣XX:T: pace-silks
  1097→jjb:HALLMARK:₣XX:D: old-coronet → new-coronet
  1098→jjb:HALLMARK:₣XX:R: heat-silks
  1099→----
  1100→
  1101→Heat-level action codes:
  1102→
  1103→* `N` — Nominate: create new heat
  1104→* `S` — Slate: add new pace
  1105→* `r` — rail: reorder paces (lowercase)
  1106→* `T` — Tally: add tack to pace
  1107→* `D` — Draft: move pace between heats (uppercase, rare)
  1108→* `R` — Retire: archive heat (uppercase)

*Landing marker* (created when spawned agent completes):
----
jjb:HALLMARK:₢XXXXX:L: {agent} {landed|crashed} | {steps_completed}/{steps_total} | verify: {pass|fail}

Files touched:
- file1
- file2

Duration: {elapsed_time}
{error_message if crashed}

Co-Authored-By: ...
----

Subject format: `{agent} {landed|crashed} | {steps_completed}/{steps_total} | verify: {pass|fail}` where agent is the executor name, landed/crashed indicates success/failure, steps show progress, and verify indicates post-execution validation status. Body contains files touched, duration, and error message if crashed.
